Title: Triose phosphate export from chloroplasts and cellular sugar content regulate anthocyanin biosynthesis during high light acclimation

Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mutant analysis (adg1-1); double mutant with tpt-2; KIN10ox interaction; sugar and anthocyanin measurements; qPCR
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not a direct anthocyanin pathway gene but a core metabolic gene experimentally used to elevate sugar levels and thereby enhance anthocyanin biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Manipulating starch partitioning can increase sugar-dependent anthocyanin production, but major carbon allocation penalties are likely
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: adg1-1 accumulates more sugars and about twofold more anthocyanin under HL; this enhancement is suppressed by tpt-2 or KIN10 overexpression
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: ADP-glucose pyrophosphorylase small/large subunit complex component of starch synthesis pathway
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Loss of ADG1 blocks starch synthesis and causes sugar overaccumulation during HL. 2. Elevated sugar status enhances PAP1/LDOX expression and anthocyanin accumulation. 3. This enhancement depends on TPT and is antagonized by KIN10 overexpression.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: high light; tpt-2 combination; KIN10 overexpression

Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Heterologous bacterial trehalose-6-phosphate phosphatase used to lower T6P and thereby increase SnRK1 activity
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Demonstrates that altering T6P/SnRK1 signaling modulates anthocyanins, though bacterial transgene deployment is mainly mechanistic
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: otsB expression diminishes PAP1/LDOX induction and reduces anthocyanin accumulation during HL
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Lowering T6P via otsB suppresses anthocyanin branch activation in HL. 2. PAP1 and LDOX induction are reduced. 3. Results support a model in which T6P-mediated repression of SnRK1 permits anthocyanin biosynthesis.

Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key late biosynthetic gene for anthocyanidin formation; not explicitly established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: high light acclimation; sucrose feeding; low CO2; DCMU; SnRK1 perturbation; AZD8055
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as a principal readout of anthocyanin branch activation; HL induction is delayed in tpt-2 and enhanced when SnRK1 is suppressed
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: increased expression accompanies and supports anthocyanin accumulation during HL; reduced/delayed expression correlates with low anthocyanin in tpt-2 and KIN10ox
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful marker and engineering target for boosting anthocyanin accumulation under light or sugar-promoting conditions, especially combined with regulatory genes such as PAP1
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: strongly induced by HL in leaves; delayed in tpt-2; enhanced by sucrose and SnRK1 suppression
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qPCR kinetics; mutant analysis; chemical and feeding treatments
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LDOX is a central late biosynthetic gene reporting activation of the anthocyanin pathway during HL. 2. Its induction requires carbon fixation, TPT-dependent photoassimilate export, and sugar accumulation. 3. SnRK1 activity represses its HL induction, whereas SnRK1 suppression or sucrose feeding enhances expression.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: oxidizes leucoanthocyanidins to anthocyanidins in the late anthocyanin pathway
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate precursor/direct aglycone of target product
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: photoprotection and antioxidant defense during stress/high light acclimation

Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key late biosynthetic gene; not explicitly established as rate-limiting in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Measured mainly in transcript kinetics/RNA-seq as a late biosynthetic gene co-induced with PAP1 and LDOX
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: its delayed HL induction in tpt-2 is associated with delayed anthocyanin accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate pathway target for anthocyanin enhancement, especially together with upstream sugar-signaling regulators
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: rapidly induced by HL in WT; delayed in tpt-2
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: time-course transcript analysis; RNA-seq comparison between WT and tpt-2
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DFR belongs to the HL-induced late anthocyanin biosynthetic module. 2. Its induction is delayed when chloroplast triose phosphate export is blocked. 3. The timing of DFR induction parallels anthocyanin accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate precursor for downstream anthocyanidin synthesis

Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: high light; chloroplast carbon fixation status
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although not a TF, TPT is a core causal gene because the tpt-2 mutant is central to the Results and demonstrates the requirement of triose phosphate export for sugar-dependent anthocyanin induction
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: high light; sucrose feeding; low CO2; DCMU
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: controls precursor export and thereby sugar-signal generation that permits transcriptional activation of anthocyanin genes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: loss of TPT strongly reduces and delays HL-induced anthocyanin accumulation and delays PAP1, DFR, and LDOX induction; sucrose feeding rescues the phenotype
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: essential upstream metabolic gate linking chloroplast carbon export to anthocyanin pathway activation during HL
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TPT-mediated triose phosphate export is required for rapid sugar accumulation during HL. 2. tpt-2 shows delayed induction of anthocyanin regulators and pathway genes and strongly reduced anthocyanin accumulation. 3. Exogenous sucrose fully rescues the anthocyanin defect, demonstrating that TPT acts through sugar availability/signaling.
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: permits induction of PAP1; TT8; DFR; LDOX and proper HL-responsive sugar gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: tpt-2 shows altered starch/sugar partitioning and delayed HL acclimation transcriptome responses
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: PAP1; TT8; DFR; LDOX; sugar-responsive transcriptome
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: photoprotection and antioxidant defense during HL acclimation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: knockout mutant analysis (tpt-2); sugar quantification; anthocyanin measurement; qPCR; RNA-seq; sucrose rescue; double mutant analysis with adg1-1
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: functional during HL acclimation; expression not central
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Manipulating chloroplast carbon export or paired sugar signaling could modulate stress-induced anthocyanin accumulation, but may affect central carbon partitioning
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: precursor export and sugar signaling upstream of flavonoid biosynthesis

Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: high light; sucrose/cellular sugar status; TPT-dependent carbon export; SnRK1 activity; low CO2 and DCMU negatively affect induction
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Presented as the central transcription factor of the anthocyanin branch and major readout for sugar/SnRK1-dependent regulation during HL
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: high light; sucrose feeding; low CO2; DCMU; KIN10 overexpression; otsB overexpression; SnRK1 knockdown; TOR inhibitor AZD8055
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional activation of late anthocyanin biosynthetic genes within the MBW module
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes HL-induced anthocyanin biosynthesis; expression is delayed/reduced in tpt-2 and KIN10ox, but enhanced in adg1-1 and SnRK1-suppressed plants
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: central positive regulator connecting sugar/SnRK1 status to anthocyanin branch activation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PAP1 is rapidly induced by HL in WT and correlates with anthocyanin accumulation. 2. Its induction depends on carbon fixation, TPT-mediated sugar export, and sufficient cellular sugar content. 3. SnRK1 activity represses PAP1, while SnRK1 suppression or sucrose feeding promotes PAP1 induction and anthocyanin accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates LDOX and other late biosynthetic genes such as DFR; coordinated with TT8
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: MBW complex with TT8 and TTG1
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: LDOX; DFR; anthocyanin late biosynthetic genes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qPCR kinetics; mutant comparisons; sucrose rescue; low CO2/DCMU treatment; KIN10 overexpression; otsB overexpression; snrk1a1-3 amiRNAi KIN11 induction
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: strongly induced in HL-treated leaves; delayed in tpt-2; enhanced in adg1-1 and SnRK1 knockdown
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong engineering target to elevate anthocyanins, especially combined with sugar-signaling manipulations; caution that broad flavonoid network and growth effects may occur

Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: cellular sugar/energy deprivation; likely repressed by sugar and trehalose-6-phosphate during HL
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: A catalytic SnRK1 subunit; overexpression represses anthocyanin branch induction during HL and partially suppresses the high-anthocyanin adg1-1 phenotype
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: high light; high sugar status in adg1-1; otsB-mediated T6P depletion
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: negative regulation of anthocyanin biosynthesis via transcriptional repression of PAP1/TT8/LDOX-associated module
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: overexpression reduces PAP1, TT8 and LDOX induction and lowers anthocyanin accumulation by ~70% in HL
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: major negative controller whose inactivation is required for full HL-induced anthocyanin biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. KIN10 overexpression compromises HL induction of the anthocyanin branch but has little effect on early genes CHS and FLS1. 2. KIN10 acts downstream of chloroplast-derived sugar signals because overexpression suppresses anthocyanin activation in adg1-1. 3. Transcriptome overlap between tpt-2 and KIN10ox supports KIN10/SnRK1 as the downstream repressor.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: not differentially expressed between WT and tpt-2 during HL
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: represses PAP1; TT8; LDOX and broader sugar-responsive transcript sets
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: likely broad metabolic and growth effects due to central energy signaling function
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: PAP1; TT8; LDOX; sugar/starvation-responsive genes such as ADK1, TPS8, DIN10
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: overexpression lines; qPCR; anthocyanin phenotyping; transcriptome comparison; double mutant with adg1-1
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: transgene overexpression in KIN10ox lines; endogenous transcript not strongly changed
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: mRNA-seq comparison with published KIN10ox data; GEO:GSE196053
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Reducing KIN10/SnRK1 activity could enhance anthocyanins under stress or controlled environments, but strong pleiotropic risks are expected
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: energy/sugar signaling control over anthocyanin biosynthesis

Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: cellular sugar/energy status; β-estradiol used experimentally to induce knockdown
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: snrk1a1-3 amiRNAi KIN11 line in Arabidopsis
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Assayed together with snrk1a1-3 to achieve partial SnRK1 suppression because constitutive double loss is lethal
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: high light; sucrose feeding; β-estradiol induction
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: negative regulation; knockdown derepresses anthocyanin branch genes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: induced knockdown increases PAP1 and LDOX expression and elevates anthocyanin accumulation under HL, even reducing dependence on sucrose feeding
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: functional evidence that lowering SnRK1 activity is sufficient to promote HL-induced anthocyanin biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Partial suppression of KIN11 in a snrk1a1 mutant background elevates anthocyanin accumulation in HL. 2. PAP1 and LDOX are more strongly induced when SnRK1 activity is reduced. 3. SnRK1 knockdown makes plants less dependent on external sucrose for anthocyanin induction.
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: repression relieved for PAP1 and LDOX upon knockdown
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: complete constitutive loss is not viable; indicates strong pleiotropic consequences
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: PAP1; LDOX; DIN10 as marker of SnRK1 activity
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: inducible amiRNA knockdown; qPCR; anthocyanin phenotyping; sucrose feeding comparison
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Partial/conditional reduction of SnRK1 activity could be used to enhance anthocyanins, but viability and growth tradeoffs require careful control
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: energy/sugar signaling control over anthocyanin biosynthesis

Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: high light; sugar status; SnRK1 activity
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: A positive MBW complex component; measured as a late anthocyanin regulator and repressed by KIN10 overexpression
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: high light; KIN10 overexpression; TPT deficiency
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional activation as part of MBW complex
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: expression is induced during HL and reduced by KIN10 overexpression; late-induced in tpt-2 transcriptome
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: important positive regulator of late anthocyanin genes in the MBW complex
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TT8 is part of the HL-induced anthocyanin regulatory module. 2. Its expression is delayed in tpt-2 and repressed by KIN10 overexpression. 3. These data support SnRK1-sensitive control of MBW-mediated anthocyanin activation.
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates late anthocyanin genes with PAP1/TTG1
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: MBW complex with PAP1 and TTG1
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: HL-induced in leaves; reduced in KIN10ox; delayed in tpt-2
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be stacked with PAP1 or sugar-signaling edits to elevate anthocyanins
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: transcriptional activation of late anthocyanin biosynthesis
